blurb: iMessage provides end-to-end encryption using a per-message AES-128 encryption key wrapped with each recipient's registered public key from the Apple Identity Service. Each device generates its own encryption (RSA-1280 or EC P-256) and signing (ECDSA-256) key pair; private keys are stored in the Secure Enclave or device Keychain. Messages are signed and encrypted before leaving the sender's device – Apple's servers relay ciphertext only. The PQ3 protocol upgrade incorporates ML-KEM post-quantum key encapsulation alongside ECDH, with a Diffie-Hellman ratchet providing forward secrecy against both classical and quantum adversaries.
